1→---
     2→title: Technology Stack Recommendations
     3→category: implementation
     4→difficulty: intermediate
     5→tags: [tech-stack, typescript, python, postgresql, architecture]
     6→related_docs: [02-data-model, 03-api-design]
     7→summary: Recommended technology stack for fleet maintenance scheduling systems with rationale for each choice.
     8→version: 1.0
     9→last_updated: 2025-12-07
    10→---
    11→
    12→# Technology Stack Recommendations
    13→
    14→This document provides technology recommendations for building a fleet maintenance scheduling system, with rationale for each choice.
    15→
    16→## Stack Overview
    17→
    18→```typescript
    19→interface RecommendedStack {
    20→  backend: {
    21→    primary: 'TypeScript + Node.js (Bun runtime)';
    22→    solver: 'Python (HiGHS + OR-Tools)';
    23→    api: 'Hono framework';
    24→  };
    25→
    26→  database: {
    27→    primary: 'PostgreSQL 16+';
    28→    timeSeries: 'TimescaleDB extension';
    29→    cache: 'Redis';
    30→  };
    31→
    32→  frontend: {
    33→    framework: 'React 18+ with TypeScript';
    34→    meta: 'Next.js or Astro';
    35→    visualization: 'ECharts';
    36→  };
    37→
    38→  infrastructure: {
    39→    containers: 'Docker';
    40→    orchestration: 'Docker Compose or Kubernetes';
    41→    cloud: 'AWS/GCP/Azure agnostic';
    42→  };
    43→}
    44→```
    45→
    46→## Backend: TypeScript + Bun
    47→
    48→### Why TypeScript
    49→
    50→```typescript
    51→interface TypeScriptBenefits {
    52→  typeSafety: 'Catch errors at compile time';
    53→  developer: 'Excellent IDE support, refactoring';
    54→  ecosystem: 'Largest package ecosystem (npm)';
    55→  hiring: 'Large developer pool';
    56→  fullStack: 'Same language frontend and backend';
    57→}
    58→```
    59→
    60→### Why Bun Runtime
    61→
    62→```typescript
    63→interface BunBenefits {
    64→  performance: '3-4x faster than Node.js';
    65→  native: 'TypeScript without transpilation';
    66→  bundler: 'Built-in bundler and test runner';
    67→  compatibility: 'Node.js API compatible';
    68→  modern: 'ESM-first, Web APIs built-in';
    69→}
    70→```
    71→
    72→### Setup Example
    73→
    74→```bash
    75→# Initialize Bun project
    76→bun init
    77→
    78→# Install dependencies
    79→bun add hono drizzle-orm postgres zod
    80→bun add -d typescript @types/node
    81→```
    82→
    83→```typescript
    84→// src/index.ts
    85→import { Hono } from 'hono';
    86→import { cors } from 'hono/cors';
    87→import { logger } from 'hono/logger';
    88→
    89→const app = new Hono();
    90→
    91→app.use('*', logger());
    92→app.use('*', cors());
    93→
    94→app.get('/health', (c) => c.json({ status: 'ok' }));
    95→
    96→export default {
    97→  port: process.env.PORT ?? 3000,
    98→  fetch: app.fetch
    99→};
   100→```
   101→
   102→## Solver: Python
   103→
   104→### Why Python for Solver
   105→
   106→```typescript
   107→interface PythonSolverBenefits {
   108→  libraries: 'Best optimization library support';
   109→  highs: 'Native highspy bindings';
   110→  ortools: 'Official Google OR-Tools support';
   111→  scientific: 'NumPy, Pandas for data processing';
   112→  academic: 'Optimization research uses Python';
   113→}
   114→```
   115→
   116→### Integration Pattern
   117→
   118→```typescript
   119→// TypeScript calls Python solver via subprocess
   120→import { spawn } from 'bun';
   121→
   122→interface SolverInput {
   123→  events: MaintenanceEvent[];
   124→  periods: number[];
   125→  capacity: number;
   126→  timeLimit: number;
   127→}
   128→
   129→interface SolverOutput {
   130→  status: string;
   131→  schedule: Record<string, number>;
   132→  objective: number;
   133→  solveTime: number;
   134→}
   135→
   136→async function callSolver(input: SolverInput): Promise<SolverOutput> {
   137→  const proc = spawn(['python3', 'solver/main.py'], {
   138→    stdin: 'pipe',
   139→    stdout: 'pipe',
   140→    stderr: 'pipe'
   141→  });
   142→
   143→  // Send input
   144→  proc.stdin.write(JSON.stringify(input));
   145→  proc.stdin.end();
   146→
   147→  // Read output
   148→  const stdout = await new Response(proc.stdout).text();
   149→  const stderr = await new Response(proc.stderr).text();
   150→
   151→  const exitCode = await proc.exited;
   152→  if (exitCode !== 0) {
   153→    throw new Error(`Solver failed: ${stderr}`);
   154→  }
   155→
   156→  return JSON.parse(stdout);
   157→}
   158→```
   159→
   160→```python
   161→# solver/main.py
   162→import sys
   163→import json
   164→import highspy
   165→
   166→def solve(input_data: dict) -> dict:
   167→    # Build and solve model
   168→    h = highspy.Highs()
   169→    h.silent()
   170→
   171→    # ... model building ...
   172→
   173→    h.run()
   174→
   175→    return {
   176→        'status': str(h.getModelStatus()),
   177→        'schedule': extract_schedule(h),
   178→        'objective': h.getInfo().objective_function_value,
   179→        'solveTime': h.getInfo().run_time
   180→    }
   181→
   182→if __name__ == '__main__':
   183→    input_data = json.load(sys.stdin)
   184→    result = solve(input_data)
   185→    print(json.dumps(result))
   186→```
   187→
   188→## Database: PostgreSQL
   189→
   190→### Why PostgreSQL
   191→
   192→```typescript
   193→interface PostgreSQLBenefits {
   194→  reliability: 'Battle-tested, ACID compliant';
   195→  features: 'JSON, arrays, CTEs, window functions';
   196→  extensions: 'TimescaleDB, PostGIS, pg_cron';
   197→  performance: 'Excellent query optimizer';
   198→  operational: 'Well-understood operations';
   199→}
   200→```
   201→
   202→### Key Extensions
   203→
   204→```sql
   205→-- TimescaleDB for time-series metrics
   206→CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS timescaledb;
   207→
   208→-- Enable for mileage history table
   209→SELECT create_hypertable('mileage_readings', 'recorded_at');
   210→
   211→-- pg_cron for scheduled jobs
   212→CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_cron;
   213→
   214→-- Schedule daily RUC calculation
   215→SELECT cron.schedule('daily-ruc', '0 5 * * *',
   216→  $$SELECT calculate_ruc()$$
   217→);
   218→```
   219→
   220→### Schema Example
   221→
   222→```typescript
   223→// drizzle/schema.ts
   224→import { pgTable, uuid, text, integer, timestamp, real } from 'drizzle-orm/pg-core';
   225→
   226→export const assets = pgTable('assets', {
   227→  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
   228→  externalId: text('external_id').unique(),
   229→  name: text('name').notNull(),
   230→  assetType: text('asset_type').notNull(),
   231→  currentMileage: integer('current_mileage').default(0),
   232→  status: text('status').default('active'),
   233→  createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
   234→  updatedAt: timestamp('updated_at').defaultNow()
   235→});
   236→
   237→export const maintenanceTypes = pgTable('maintenance_types', {
   238→  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
   239→  name: text('name').notNull(),
   240→  intervalMileage: integer('interval_mileage'),
   241→  intervalDays: integer('interval_days'),
   242→  durationHours: real('duration_hours').notNull(),
   243→  manHours: real('man_hours').notNull()
   244→});
   245→
   246→export const scheduledEvents = pgTable('scheduled_events', {
   247→  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
   248→  assetId: uuid('asset_id').references(() => assets.id),
   249→  maintenanceTypeId: uuid('maintenance_type_id').references(() => maintenanceTypes.id),
   250→  scheduledWeek: integer('scheduled_week').notNull(),
   251→  targetMileage: integer('target_mileage'),
   252→  status: text('status').default('scheduled'),
   253→  scheduleVersion: integer('schedule_version').notNull()
   254→});
   255→```
   256→
   257→## Cache: Redis
   258→
   259→### Why Redis
   260→
   261→```typescript
   262→interface RedisBenefits {
   263→  speed: 'Microsecond latency';
   264→  versatile: 'Cache, queue, pub/sub, sessions';
   265→  simple: 'Easy to operate';
   266→  scalable: 'Cluster mode for HA';
   267→}
   268→```
   269→
   270→### Use Cases
   271→
   272→```typescript
   273→// Session caching
   274→await redis.set(`session:${userId}`, JSON.stringify(session), 'EX', 3600);
   275→
   276→// API response caching
   277→const cached = await redis.get(`schedule:${scheduleId}`);
   278→if (cached) return JSON.parse(cached);
   279→
   280→// Job queue
   281→await redis.lpush('solver:jobs', JSON.stringify(job));
   282→
   283→// Pub/sub for real-time updates
   284→redis.publish('schedule:updated', JSON.stringify({ scheduleId }));
   285→```
   286→
   287→## Frontend: React + Next.js
   288→
   289→### Why React
   290→
   291→```typescript
   292→interface ReactBenefits {
   293→  ecosystem: 'Largest component ecosystem';
   294→  hiring: 'Most developers know React';
   295→  tooling: 'Excellent development tools';
   296→  flexibility: 'Works with any backend';
   297→}
   298→```
   299→
   300→### Why Next.js
   301→
   302→```typescript
   303→interface NextJSBenefits {
   304→  ssr: 'Server-side rendering for SEO and performance';
   305→  routing: 'File-based routing';
   306→  api: 'API routes for BFF pattern';
   307→  optimization: 'Image, font, script optimization';
   308→  deployment: 'Easy deployment to Vercel or self-host';
   309→}
   310→```

   313→

   335→## Visualization: ECharts
   336→
   337→### Why ECharts
   338→
   339→```typescript
   340→interface EChartsBenefits {
   341→  performance: 'Handles 100k+ data points';
   342→  features: 'Gantt, heatmaps, calendars';
   343→  customization: 'Highly configurable';
   344→  opensource: 'Apache 2.0 license';
   345→}
   346→```
   347→
   348→### Schedule Visualization Example
   349→
   350→```typescript
   351→import ReactECharts from 'echarts-for-react';
   352→
   353→function ScheduleGantt({ events }: { events: ScheduledEvent[] }) {
   354→  const option = {
   355→    tooltip: { trigger: 'item' },
   356→    xAxis: {
   357→      type: 'time',
   358→      min: startDate,
   359→      max: endDate
   360→    },
   361→    yAxis: {
   362→      type: 'category',
   363→      data: assets.map(a => a.name)
   364→    },
   365→    series: [{
   366→      type: 'custom',
   367→      renderItem: renderGanttBar,
   368→      encode: { x: [0, 1], y: 2 },
   369→      data: events.map(e => [
   370→        e.startDate,
   371→        e.endDate,
   372→        e.assetIndex,
   373→        e.maintenanceType
   374→      ])
   375→    }]
   376→  };
   377→
   378→  return <ReactECharts option={option} style={{ height: 600 }} />;
   379→}
   380→```

    11→
    12→# Deployment Guide
    13→
    14→This document covers deployment strategies for fleet maintenance scheduling systems, from development to production.
    15→
    16→## Deployment Architecture
    17→
    18→### Production Topology
    19→
    20→```
    21→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    22→│                             CDN (CloudFront/Cloudflare)                  │
    23→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    24→                                    │
    25→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    26→│                          Load Balancer (ALB/nginx)                       │
    27→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    28→          │                         │                         │
    29→          ▼                         ▼                         ▼
    30→┌─────────────────┐      ┌─────────────────┐      ┌─────────────────┐
    31→│    API Pod 1    │      │    API Pod 2    │      │    API Pod 3    │
    32→│   (Bun + Hono)  │      │   (Bun + Hono)  │      │   (Bun + Hono)  │
    33→└─────────────────┘      └─────────────────┘      └─────────────────┘
    34→          │                         │                         │
    35→          └─────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────┘
    36→                                    │
    37→          ┌─────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────┐
    38→          │                         │                         │
    39→          ▼                         ▼                         ▼
    40→┌─────────────────┐      ┌─────────────────┐      ┌─────────────────┐
    41→│  Solver Worker  │      │  Solver Worker  │      │  Solver Worker  │
    42→│    (Python)     │      │    (Python)     │      │    (Python)     │
    43→└─────────────────┘      └─────────────────┘      └─────────────────┘
    44→                                    │
    45→┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    46→│                          Data Layer                                    │
    47→│  ┌──────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐                │
    48→│  │  PostgreSQL  │  │    Redis     │  │      S3      │                │
    49→│  │  (Primary)   │  │   Cluster    │  │    Bucket    │                │
    50→│  └──────────────┘  └──────────────┘  └──────────────┘                │
    51→└───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    52→```
    53→
    54→## Docker Configuration
    55→
    56→### API Dockerfile
    57→
    58→```dockerfile
    59→# Dockerfile.api
    60→FROM oven/bun:1.1 AS base
    61→WORKDIR /app
    62→
    63→# Install dependencies
    64→FROM base AS deps
    65→COPY package.json bun.lockb ./
    66→RUN bun install --frozen-lockfile --production
    67→
    68→# Build
    69→FROM base AS builder
    70→COPY package.json bun.lockb ./
    71→RUN bun install --frozen-lockfile
    72→COPY . .
    73→RUN bun run build
    74→
    75→# Production image
    76→FROM base AS runner
    77→ENV NODE_ENV=production
    78→
    79→RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 nodejs
    80→RUN adduser --system --uid 1001 bunjs
    81→
    82→COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
    83→COPY --from=builder /app/dist ./dist
    84→COPY --from=builder /app/package.json ./
    85→
    86→USER bunjs
    87→
    88→EXPOSE 3000
    89→
    90→CMD ["bun", "run", "dist/index.js"]
    91→```
    92→
    93→### Solver Dockerfile
    94→
    95→```dockerfile
    96→# Dockerfile.solver
    97→FROM python:3.12-slim AS base
    98→
    99→WORKDIR /app
   100→
   101→# Install system dependencies
   102→RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
   103→    gcc \
   104→    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
   105→
   106→# Install Python dependencies
   107→COPY requirements.txt .
   108→RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt
   109→
   110→# Copy application
   111→COPY solver/ ./solver/
   112→
   113→# Non-root user
   114→RUN useradd -m -u 1001 solver
   115→USER solver
   116→
   117→CMD ["python", "-m", "solver.worker"]
   118→```
   119→
   120→### Docker Compose (Development)
   121→
   122→```yaml
   123→# docker-compose.yml
   124→version: '3.8'
   125→
   126→services:
   127→  api:
   128→    build:
   129→      context: .
   130→      dockerfile: Dockerfile.api
   131→    ports:
   132→      - "3000:3000"
   133→    environment:
   134→      DATABASE_URL: postgresql://postgres:postgres@db:5432/mms
   135→      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379
   136→      NODE_ENV: development
   137→    volumes:
   138→      - ./apps/api/src:/app/src:ro
   139→    depends_on:
   140→      db:
   141→        condition: service_healthy
   142→      redis:
   143→        condition: service_started
   144→
   145→  solver:
   146→    build:
   147→      context: .
   148→      dockerfile: Dockerfile.solver
   149→    environment:
   150→      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379
   151→      DATABASE_URL: postgresql://postgres:postgres@db:5432/mms
   152→    depends_on:
   153→      - redis
   154→      - db
   155→
   156→  web:
   157→    build:
   158→      context: ./apps/web
   159→      dockerfile: Dockerfile
   160→    ports:
   161→      - "3001:3000"
   162→    environment:
   163→      NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL: http://localhost:3000
   164→
   165→  db:
   166→    image: timescale/timescaledb:latest-pg16
   167→    ports:
   168→      - "5432:5432"
   169→    environment:
   170→      POSTGRES_USER: postgres
   171→      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres
   172→      POSTGRES_DB: mms
   173→    volumes:
   174→      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data
   175→      - ./scripts/init.sql:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init.sql
   176→    healthcheck:
   177→      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U postgres"]
   178→      interval: 5s
   179→      timeout: 5s
   180→      retries: 5
   181→
   182→  redis:
   183→    image: redis:7-alpine
   184→    ports:
   185→      - "6379:6379"
   186→    volumes:
   187→      - redisdata:/data
   188→
   189→volumes:
   190→  pgdata:
   191→  redisdata:
   192→```
   193→
   194→## Kubernetes Deployment
   195→
   196→### API Deployment
   197→
   198→```yaml
   199→# k8s/api-deployment.yaml
   200→apiVersion: apps/v1
   201→kind: Deployment
   202→metadata:
   203→  name: mms-api
   204→  labels:
   205→    app: mms-api
   206→spec:
   207→  replicas: 3
   208→  selector:
   209→    matchLabels:
   210→      app: mms-api
   211→  template:
   212→    metadata:
   213→      labels:
   214→        app: mms-api
   215→    spec:
   216→      containers:
   217→        - name: api
   218→          image: your-registry/mms-api:latest
   219→          ports:
   220→            - containerPort: 3000
   221→          env:
   222→            - name: DATABASE_URL
   223→              valueFrom:
   224→                secretKeyRef:
   225→                  name: mms-secrets
   226→                  key: database-url
   227→            - name: REDIS_URL
   228→              valueFrom:
   229→                secretKeyRef:
   230→                  name: mms-secrets
   231→                  key: redis-url
   232→          resources:
   233→            requests:
   234→              memory: "256Mi"
   235→              cpu: "100m"
   236→            limits:
   237→              memory: "512Mi"
   238→              cpu: "500m"
   239→          livenessProbe:
   240→            httpGet:
   241→              path: /health
   242→              port: 3000
   243→            initialDelaySeconds: 10
   244→            periodSeconds: 10
   245→          readinessProbe:
   246→            httpGet:
   247→              path: /health
   248→              port: 3000
   249→            initialDelaySeconds: 5
   250→            periodSeconds: 5
   251→---
   252→apiVersion: v1
   253→kind: Service
   254→metadata:
   255→  name: mms-api
   256→spec:
   257→  selector:
   258→    app: mms-api
   259→  ports:
   260→    - port: 80
   261→      targetPort: 3000
   262→  type: ClusterIP
   263→```

   308→### Horizontal Pod Autoscaler
   309→

   331→
   332→## Cloud Platform Deployment
   333→
   334→### AWS with ECS/Fargate
   335→
   336→```typescript
   337→// infrastructure/aws-cdk/stack.ts
   338→import * as cdk from 'aws-cdk-lib';
   339→import * as ecs from 'aws-cdk-lib/aws-ecs';
   340→import * as ec2 from 'aws-cdk-lib/aws-ec2';
   341→import * as rds from 'aws-cdk-lib/aws-rds';
   342→
   343→export class MMSStack extends cdk.Stack {
   344→  constructor(scope: cdk.App, id: string, props?: cdk.StackProps) {
   345→    super(scope, id, props);
   346→
   347→    // VPC
   348→    const vpc = new ec2.Vpc(this, 'MmsVpc', {
   349→      maxAzs: 2
   350→    });
   351→
   352→    // ECS Cluster
   353→    const cluster = new ecs.Cluster(this, 'MmsCluster', {
   354→      vpc,
   355→      containerInsights: true
   356→    });
   357→
   358→    // RDS PostgreSQL
   359→    const database = new rds.DatabaseInstance(this, 'MmsDatabase', {
   360→      engine: rds.DatabaseInstanceEngine.postgres({
   361→        version: rds.PostgresEngineVersion.VER_16
   362→      }),
   363→      instanceType: ec2.InstanceType.of(
   364→        ec2.InstanceClass.T3,
   365→        ec2.InstanceSize.MEDIUM
   366→      ),
   367→      vpc,
   368→      databaseName: 'mms'
   369→    });
   370→
   371→    // API Service
   372→    const apiService = new ecs.FargateService(this, 'MmsApiService', {
   373→      cluster,
   374→      taskDefinition: this.createApiTaskDefinition(),
   375→      desiredCount: 2
   376→    });
   377→
   378→    // Solver Service
   379→    const solverService = new ecs.FargateService(this, 'MmsSolverService', {
   380→      cluster,
   381→      taskDefinition: this.createSolverTaskDefinition(),
   382→      desiredCount: 2
   383→    });
   384→  }
   385→}
   386→```
   387→
   388→### Environment Configuration
   389→
   390→```typescript
   391→// config/env.ts
   392→import { z } from 'zod';
   393→
   394→const envSchema = z.object({
   395→  NODE_ENV: z.enum(['development', 'staging', 'production']),
   396→  PORT: z.coerce.number().default(3000),
   397→
   398→  // Database
   399→  DATABASE_URL: z.string().url(),
   400→  DATABASE_POOL_SIZE: z.coerce.number().default(10),
   401→
   402→  // Redis
   403→  REDIS_URL: z.string().url(),
   404→
   405→  // Auth
   406→  JWT_SECRET: z.string().min(32),
   407→  JWT_EXPIRY: z.string().default('24h'),
   408→
   409→  // External APIs
   410→  EAM_API_URL: z.string().url().optional(),
   411→  EAM_API_KEY: z.string().optional(),
   412→
   413→  // Observability
   414→  LOG_LEVEL: z.enum(['debug', 'info', 'warn', 'error']).default('info'),
   415→  SENTRY_DSN: z.string().url().optional()
   416→});
   417→
   418→export const env = envSchema.parse(process.env);
   419→```

   421→## CI/CD Pipeline
   422→
   423→### GitHub Actions
   424→
   425→```yaml
   426→# .github/workflows/deploy.yml
   427→name: Deploy
   428→
   429→on:
   430→  push:
   431→    branches: [main]
   432→
   433→jobs:
   434→  test:
   435→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
   436→    steps:
   437→      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
   438→
   439→      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v1
   440→        with:
   441→          bun-version: latest
   442→
   443→      - run: bun install
   444→      - run: bun test
   445→      - run: bun run lint
   446→
   447→  build-and-push:
   448→    needs: test
   449→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
   450→    steps:
   451→      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
   452→
   453→      - uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3
   454→
   455→      - uses: docker/login-action@v3
   456→        with:
   457→          registry: ghcr.io
   458→          username: ${{ github.actor }}
   459→          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
   460→
   461→      - uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
   462→        with:
   463→          context: .
   464→          file: ./Dockerfile.api
   465→          push: true
   466→          tags: ghcr.io/${{ github.repository }}/api:${{ github.sha }}
   467→          cache-from: type=gha
   468→          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max
   469→
   470→  deploy:
   471→    needs: build-and-push
   472→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
   473→    steps:
   474→      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
   475→
   476→      - name: Deploy to Kubernetes
   477→        uses: azure/k8s-deploy@v4
   478→        with:
   479→          manifests: |
   480→            k8s/api-deployment.yaml
   481→            k8s/solver-deployment.yaml
   482→          images: |
   483→            ghcr.io/${{ github.repository }}/api:${{ github.sha }}
   484→```
   485→
   486→## Monitoring and Observability
   487→
   488→### Structured Logging
   489→
   490→```typescript
   491→// lib/logger.ts
   492→import pino from 'pino';
   493→
   494→export const logger = pino({
   495→  level: process.env.LOG_LEVEL ?? 'info',
   496→  transport: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development'
   497→    ? { target: 'pino-pretty' }
   498→    : undefined,
   499→  formatters: {
   500→    level: (label) => ({ level: label })
   501→  }
   502→});
   503→```
   504→
   505→### Health Check Endpoint
   506→
   507→```typescript
   508→// routes/health.ts
   509→app.get('/health', async (c) => {
   510→  const checks = {
   511→    database: await checkDatabase(),
   512→    redis: await checkRedis(),
   513→    solver: await checkSolverQueue()
   514→  };
   515→
   516→  const healthy = Object.values(checks).every(c => c.status === 'healthy');
   517→
   518→  return c.json({
   519→    status: healthy ? 'healthy' : 'unhealthy',
   520→    timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
   521→    checks
   522→  }, healthy ? 200 : 503);
   523→});
   524→```
   525→
   526→### Prometheus Metrics
   527→
   528→```typescript
   529→// middleware/metrics.ts
   530→import { Registry, Counter, Histogram } from 'prom-client';
   531→
   532→const registry = new Registry();
   533→
   534→export const httpRequestsTotal = new Counter({
   535→  name: 'http_requests_total',
   536→  help: 'Total HTTP requests',
   537→  labelNames: ['method', 'path', 'status'],
   538→  registers: [registry]
   539→});
   540→
   541→export const httpRequestDuration = new Histogram({
   542→  name: 'http_request_duration_seconds',
   543→  help: 'HTTP request duration',
   544→  labelNames: ['method', 'path'],

   555→
   556→# Generate migration

file.content:
---
title: Deployment Guide
category: implementation
difficulty: intermediate
tags: [deployment, docker, kubernetes, cloud, devops]
related_docs: [01-tech-stack, 03-api-design]
summary: Deployment patterns for fleet maintenance scheduling systems including Docker, Kubernetes, and cloud platforms.
version: 1.0
last_updated: 2025-12-07
---

# Deployment Guide

This document covers deployment strategies for fleet maintenance scheduling systems, from development to production.

## Deployment Architecture

### Production Topology

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                             CDN (CloudFront/Cloudflare)                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                                    │
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                          Load Balancer (ALB/nginx)                       │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
          │                         │                         │
          ▼                         ▼                         ▼
┌─────────────────┐      ┌─────────────────┐      ┌─────────────────┐
│    API Pod 1    │      │    API Pod 2    │      │    API Pod 3    │
│   (Bun + Hono)  │      │   (Bun + Hono)  │      │   (Bun + Hono)  │
└─────────────────┘      └─────────────────┘      └─────────────────┘
          │                         │                         │
          └─────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────┘
                                    │
          ┌─────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────┐
          │                         │                         │
          ▼                         ▼                         ▼
┌─────────────────┐      ┌─────────────────┐      ┌─────────────────┐
│  Solver Worker  │      │  Solver Worker  │      │  Solver Worker  │
│    (Python)     │      │    (Python)     │      │    (Python)     │
└─────────────────┘      └─────────────────┘      └─────────────────┘
                                    │
┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                          Data Layer                                    │
│  ┌──────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐                │
│  │  PostgreSQL  │  │    Redis     │  │      S3      │                │
│  │  (Primary)   │  │   Cluster    │  │    Bucket    │                │
│  └──────────────┘  └──────────────┘  └──────────────┘                │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Docker Configuration

### API Dockerfile

```dockerfile
# Dockerfile.api
FROM oven/bun:1.1 AS base
WORKDIR /app

# Install dependencies
FROM base AS deps
COPY package.json bun.lockb ./
RUN bun install --frozen-lockfile --production

# Build
FROM base AS builder
COPY package.json bun.lockb ./
RUN bun install --frozen-lockfile
COPY . .
RUN bun run build

# Production image
FROM base AS runner
ENV NODE_ENV=production

RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 nodejs
RUN adduser --system --uid 1001 bunjs

COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY --from=builder /app/dist ./dist
COPY --from=builder /app/package.json ./

USER bunjs

EXPOSE 3000

CMD ["bun", "run", "dist/index.js"]
```

### Solver Dockerfile

```dockerfile
# Dockerfile.solver
FROM python:3.12-slim AS base

WORKDIR /app

# Install system dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    gcc \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Python dependencies
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

# Copy application
COPY solver/ ./solver/

# Non-root user
RUN useradd -m -u 1001 solver
USER solver

CMD ["python", "-m", "solver.worker"]
```

### Docker Compose (Development)

```yaml
# docker-compose.yml
version: '3.8'

services:
  api:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: Dockerfile.api
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgresql://postgres:postgres@db:5432/mms
      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379
      NODE_ENV: development
    volumes:
      - ./apps/api/src:/app/src:ro
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy
      redis:
        condition: service_started

  solver:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: Dockerfile.solver
    environment:
      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379
      DATABASE_URL: postgresql://postgres:postgres@db:5432/mms
    depends_on:
      - redis
      - db

  web:
    build:
      context: ./apps/web
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    ports:
      - "3001:3000"
    environment:
      NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL: http://localhost:3000

  db:
    image: timescale/timescaledb:latest-pg16
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: postgres
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres
      POSTGRES_DB: mms
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data
      - ./scripts/init.sql:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init.sql
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U postgres"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    ports:
      - "6379:6379"
    volumes:
      - redisdata:/data

volumes:
  pgdata:
  redisdata:
```

## Kubernetes Deployment

### API Deployment

```yaml
# k8s/api-deployment.yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: mms-api
  labels:
    app: mms-api
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: mms-api
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: mms-api
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: api
          image: your-registry/mms-api:latest
          ports:
            - containerPort: 3000
          env:
            - name: DATABASE_URL
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: mms-secrets
                  key: database-url
            - name: REDIS_URL
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: mms-secrets
                  key: redis-url
          resources:
            requests:
              memory: "256Mi"
              cpu: "100m"
            limits:
              memory: "512Mi"
              cpu: "500m"
          livenessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /health
              port: 3000
            initialDelaySeconds: 10
            periodSeconds: 10
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /health
              port: 3000
            initialDelaySeconds: 5
            periodSeconds: 5
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: mms-api
spec:
  selector:
    app: mms-api
  ports:
    - port: 80
      targetPort: 3000
  type: ClusterIP
```

### Solver Worker Deployment

```yaml
# k8s/solver-deployment.yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: mms-solver
  labels:
    app: mms-solver
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: mms-solver
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: mms-solver
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: solver
          image: your-registry/mms-solver:latest
          env:
            - name: DATABASE_URL
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: mms-secrets
                  key: database-url
            - name: REDIS_URL
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: mms-secrets
                  key: redis-url
          resources:
            requests:
              memory: "1Gi"
              cpu: "500m"
            limits:
              memory: "4Gi"
              cpu: "2000m"
```

### Horizontal Pod Autoscaler

```yaml
# k8s/hpa.yaml
apiVersion: autoscaling/v2
kind: HorizontalPodAutoscaler
metadata:
  name: mms-api-hpa
spec:
  scaleTargetRef:
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    name: mms-api
  minReplicas: 2
  maxReplicas: 10
  metrics:
    - type: Resource
      resource:
        name: cpu
        target:
          type: Utilization
          averageUtilization: 70
```

## Cloud Platform Deployment

### AWS with ECS/Fargate

```typescript
// infrastructure/aws-cdk/stack.ts
import * as cdk from 'aws-cdk-lib';
import * as ecs from 'aws-cdk-lib/aws-ecs';
import * as ec2 from 'aws-cdk-lib/aws-ec2';
import * as rds from 'aws-cdk-lib/aws-rds';

export class MMSStack extends cdk.Stack {
  constructor(scope: cdk.App, id: string, props?: cdk.StackProps) {
    super(scope, id, props);

    // VPC
    const vpc = new ec2.Vpc(this, 'MmsVpc', {
      maxAzs: 2
    });

    // ECS Cluster
    const cluster = new ecs.Cluster(this, 'MmsCluster', {
      vpc,
      containerInsights: true
    });

    // RDS PostgreSQL
    const database = new rds.DatabaseInstance(this, 'MmsDatabase', {
      engine: rds.DatabaseInstanceEngine.postgres({
        version: rds.PostgresEngineVersion.VER_16
      }),
      instanceType: ec2.InstanceType.of(
        ec2.InstanceClass.T3,
        ec2.InstanceSize.MEDIUM
      ),
      vpc,
      databaseName: 'mms'
    });

    // API Service
    const apiService = new ecs.FargateService(this, 'MmsApiService', {
      cluster,
      taskDefinition: this.createApiTaskDefinition(),
      desiredCount: 2
    });

    // Solver Service
    const solverService = new ecs.FargateService(this, 'MmsSolverService', {
      cluster,
      taskDefinition: this.createSolverTaskDefinition(),
      desiredCount: 2
    });
  }
}
```

### Environment Configuration

```typescript
// config/env.ts
import { z } from 'zod';

const envSchema = z.object({
  NODE_ENV: z.enum(['development', 'staging', 'production']),
  PORT: z.coerce.number().default(3000),

  // Database
  DATABASE_URL: z.string().url(),
  DATABASE_POOL_SIZE: z.coerce.number().default(10),

  // Redis
  REDIS_URL: z.string().url(),

  // Auth
  JWT_SECRET: z.string().min(32),
  JWT_EXPIRY: z.string().default('24h'),

  // External APIs
  EAM_API_URL: z.string().url().optional(),
  EAM_API_KEY: z.string().optional(),

  // Observability
  LOG_LEVEL: z.enum(['debug', 'info', 'warn', 'error']).default('info'),
  SENTRY_DSN: z.string().url().optional()
});

export const env = envSchema.parse(process.env);
```

## CI/CD Pipeline

### GitHub Actions

```yaml
# .github/workflows/deploy.yml
name: Deploy

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v1
        with:
          bun-version: latest

      - run: bun install
      - run: bun test
      - run: bun run lint

  build-and-push:
    needs: test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3

      - uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ghcr.io
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: .
          file: ./Dockerfile.api
          push: true
          tags: ghcr.io/${{ github.repository }}/api:${{ github.sha }}
          cache-from: type=gha
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max

  deploy:
    needs: build-and-push
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Deploy to Kubernetes
        uses: azure/k8s-deploy@v4
        with:
          manifests: |
            k8s/api-deployment.yaml
            k8s/solver-deployment.yaml
          images: |
            ghcr.io/${{ github.repository }}/api:${{ github.sha }}
```

## Monitoring and Observability

### Structured Logging

```typescript
// lib/logger.ts
import pino from 'pino';

export const logger = pino({
  level: process.env.LOG_LEVEL ?? 'info',
  transport: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development'
    ? { target: 'pino-pretty' }
    : undefined,
  formatters: {
    level: (label) => ({ level: label })
  }
});
```

### Health Check Endpoint

```typescript
// routes/health.ts
app.get('/health', async (c) => {
  const checks = {
    database: await checkDatabase(),
    redis: await checkRedis(),
    solver: await checkSolverQueue()
  };

  const healthy = Object.values(checks).every(c => c.status === 'healthy');

  return c.json({
    status: healthy ? 'healthy' : 'unhealthy',
    timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
    checks
  }, healthy ? 200 : 503);
});
```

### Prometheus Metrics

```typescript
// middleware/metrics.ts
import { Registry, Counter, Histogram } from 'prom-client';

const registry = new Registry();

export const httpRequestsTotal = new Counter({
  name: 'http_requests_total',
  help: 'Total HTTP requests',
  labelNames: ['method', 'path', 'status'],
  registers: [registry]
});

export const httpRequestDuration = new Histogram({
  name: 'http_request_duration_seconds',
  help: 'HTTP request duration',
  labelNames: ['method', 'path'],
  buckets: [0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.5, 1, 5],
  registers: [registry]
});
```

## Database Migrations

```bash
# Run migrations
bun run db:migrate

# Generate migration
bun run db:generate

# Push schema (development)
bun run db:push
```

```json
// package.json scripts
{
  "scripts": {
    "db:generate": "drizzle-kit generate",
    "db:migrate": "drizzle-kit migrate",
    "db:push": "drizzle-kit push",
    "db:studio": "drizzle-kit studio"
  }
}
```

## Backup and Recovery

```bash
# PostgreSQL backup script
#!/bin/bash
TIMESTAMP=$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)
BACKUP_FILE="mms_backup_${TIMESTAMP}.sql.gz"

pg_dump $DATABASE_URL | gzip > $BACKUP_FILE
aws s3 cp $BACKUP_FILE s3://mms-backups/

# Keep only last 30 days
find /backups -name "*.sql.gz" -mtime +30 -delete
```

## Next Steps

With deployment configured:

- Explore `06-FEATURES/` for feature implementation
- Study `07-CASE-STUDIES/` for production examples
- Review monitoring and alerting setup

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Repomix configuration for security, exclude patterns, and allowing specific files:

## Repomix Configuration Overview

Repomix uses a `repomix.config.json` file with several configuration sections including `security`, `ignore`, and `include` options.

### Security Configuration

When `security.enableSecurityCheck` is enabled, Repomix uses Secretlint to detect sensitive information in your codebase before including it in the output.(no content)This is described as "Security-Focused: Incorporates Secretlint for robust security checks to detect and prevent inclusion of sensitive information."(no content)You can also use `--no-security-check` to skip scanning for sensitive data like API keys and passwords.

### Ignore/Exclude Patterns

Repomix combines multiple ignore sources: `.git/info/exclude`, optional `.gitignore`, default ignore patterns, custom patterns, and explicit output files are all merged carefully to ensure accurate file filtering.

The configuration allows setting custom patterns:
```json
"ignore": {
  "useGitignore": true,
  "useDefaultPatterns": true,
  "customPatterns": ["additional-folder", "**/*.log"]
}
```

### Allowing Specific Files (Force Include)

There's a known limitation: users wanting to include specific files from excluded directories (like `node_modules/`) find that even specifying the exact path doesn't work because of default or custom exclusion rules.(no content)A feature request exists for a "force include" option that would explicitly override all exclusion rules—both default exclusions and patterns set by `.gitignore`, `.repomixignore`, or other custom rules.(no content)The proposed solution suggests adding a `forceInclude` array in `repomix.config.json` that would override all exclusions:
```json
{
  "forceInclude": [
    "node_modules/some-package/dist/specific-file.js"
  ]
}
```

There's also a request for a `--force-include` CLI option.

**Note:** This feature request was filed in March 2025, so the "force include" functionality may not be implemented yet in the current version.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Repomix configuration for security, exclude patterns, and allowing specific files:

## Repomix Configuration Overview

Repomix uses a `repomix.config.json` file with several configuration sections including `security`, `ignore`, and `include` options.

### Security Configuration

When `security.enableSecurityCheck` is enabled, Repomix uses Secretlint to detect sensitive information in your codebase before including it in the output.(no content)This is described as "Security-Focused: Incorporates Secretlint for robust security checks to detect and prevent inclusion of sensitive information."(no content)You can also use `--no-security-check` to skip scanning for sensitive data like API keys and passwords.

### Ignore/Exclude Patterns

Repomix combines multiple ignore sources: `.git/info/exclude`, optional `.gitignore`, default ignore patterns, custom patterns, and explicit output files are all merged carefully to ensure accurate file filtering.

The configuration allows setting custom patterns:
```json
"ignore": {
  "useGitignore": true,
  "useDefaultPatterns": true,
  "customPatterns": ["additional-folder", "**/*.log"]
}
```

### Allowing Specific Files (Force Include)

There's a known limitation: users wanting to include specific files from excluded directories (like `node_modules/`) find that even specifying the exact path doesn't work because of default or custom exclusion rules.(no content)A feature request exists for a "force include" option that would explicitly override all exclusion rules—both default exclusions and patterns set by `.gitignore`, `.repomixignore`, or other custom rules.(no content)The proposed solution suggests adding a `forceInclude` array in `repomix.config.json` that would override all exclusions:
```json
{
  "forceInclude": [
    "node_modules/some-package/dist/specific-file.js"
  ]
}
```

There's also a request for a `--force-include` CLI option.

**Note:** This feature request was filed in March 2025, so the "force include" functionality may not be implemented yet in the current version.

input.prompt: What are all the security configuration options? Specifically how to exclude files or patterns from the security check?

content.content:
# Security Configuration in Repomix

Based on the documentation provided, there is **only one security configuration option**:

**`security.enableSecurityCheck`** - A boolean that controls whether Repomix performs security checks using Secretlint to detect sensitive information (default: `true`).

## Important Limitation

The documentation does **not provide any method to exclude specific files or patterns from the security check**. The configuration only allows you to enable or disable security checks entirely.

When activated, the feature scans your codebase to prevent accidental exposure of "API keys, access tokens, private keys, passwords, and other sensitive credentials," but there's no granular control to whitelist certain files or patterns from this inspection.

If you need to exclude specific files from security scanning, you may need to consult the project's GitHub repository or community channels for potential workarounds or feature requests.

# Security Configuration in Repomix

Based on the documentation provided, there is **only one security configuration option**:

**`security.enableSecurityCheck`** - A boolean that controls whether Repomix performs security checks using Secretlint to detect sensitive information (default: `true`).

## Important Limitation

The documentation does **not provide any method to exclude specific files or patterns from the security check**. The configuration only allows you to enable or disable security checks entirely.

When activated, the feature scans your codebase to prevent accidental exposure of "API keys, access tokens, private keys, passwords, and other sensitive credentials," but there's no granular control to whitelist certain files or patterns from this inspection.

If you need to exclude specific files from security scanning, you may need to consult the project's GitHub repository or community channels for potential workarounds or feature requests.

content.thinking:
Também não funcionou. Parece que o repomix usa uma configuração interna do secretlint e não respeita arquivos de configuração externos.

Neste caso, temos duas opções:
1. Desabilitar completamente a verificação de segurança (não ideal)
2. Sanitizar os arquivos de documentação para remover os exemplos que parecem credenciais

A opção 2 seria a mais correta - substituir os exemplos de credenciais nos arquivos de documentação por placeholders mais óbvios que não disparem o secretlint.

Por exemplo:
- `POSTGRES_PASSWORD=postgres` → `POSTGRES_PASSWORD=<your-password>`
- `postgresql://postgres:postgres@db:5432/mms` → `postgresql://<user>:<password>@db:5432/mms`
- `JWT_SECRET: z.string().min(32)` é apenas validação, não uma credencial real

Mas antes, deixe-me verificar se há uma versão mais recente do repomix que suporte a configuração `excludePatterns`.

content.text:
O repomix não está respeitando configurações externas do Secretlint. Temos duas opções:

1. **Desabilitar a verificação** (voltando à solução anterior)
2. **Sanitizar os arquivos** - substituir exemplos de credenciais por placeholders óbvios

A opção 2 é mais correta. Posso substituir nos arquivos de doc:
- `POSTGRES_PASSWORD=postgres` → `POSTGRES_PASSWORD=<your-password>`
- `postgresql://postgres:postgres@...` → `postgresql://<user>:<password>@...`

Qual prefere?

input.prompt: Does repomix support PDF files? What file types can it process? Is there a list of supported file extensions?

content.content:
# File Type Support in Repomix

Based on the documentation provided, **Repomix does not explicitly support PDF files**. The content mentions that "Binary files (such as images, PDFs, compiled binaries, archives, etc.) are handled specially" by excluding their contents from output while listing them in the directory structure.

## What Repomix Processes

The documentation doesn't provide a definitive list of supported file extensions. However, it indicates that:

- **Text-based files** are included in the output content
- **Binary files** (including PDFs) are excluded from content but appear in directory listings
- Features like comment removal work with "supported file types," though specific extensions aren't enumerated

The tool appears designed primarily for **source code analysis**, given references to code compression using Tree-sitter and features targeting programming languages. For a complete list of supported file types and extensions, you would need to consult Repomix's GitHub repository or additional documentation beyond what's shown here.

content.thinking: O pymupdf foi instalado. Agora vou criar um script Python para extrair os PDFs para Markdown.

input.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import pymupdf
import os

pdf_files = [
    "docs/@start-here/RFQ.pdf",
    "docs/@start-here/proposal_and_sow.pdf",
    "docs/@start-here/email.pdf"
]

output_dir = "docs/@start-here"

for pdf_path in pdf_files:
    if not os.path.exists(pdf_path):
        print(f"File not found: {pdf_path}")
        continue
    
    doc = pymupdf.open(pdf_path)
    
    # Get base name without extension
    base_name = os.path.splitext(os.path.basename(pdf_path))[0]
    output_path = os.path.join(output_dir, f"{base_name}.md")
    
    # Extract text from all pages
    text_content = []
    for page_num, page in enumerate(doc, 1):
        text = page.get_text()
        if text.strip():
            text_content.append(f"## Page {page_num}\n\n{text}")
    
    doc.close()
    
    # Write markdown file
    with open(output_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write(f"# {base_name}\n\n")
        f.write(f"*Extracted from {os.path.basename(pdf_path)}*\n\n")
        f.write("---\n\n")
        f.write("\n\n".join(text_content))
    
    print(f"Extracted: {pdf_path} -> {output_path}")

print("\nDone!")
EOF

1→---
     2→title: Technology Stack Recommendations
     3→category: implementation
     4→difficulty: intermediate
     5→tags: [tech-stack, typescript, python, postgresql, architecture]
     6→related_docs: [02-data-model, 03-api-design]
     7→summary: Recommended technology stack for fleet maintenance scheduling systems with rationale for each choice.
     8→version: 1.0
     9→last_updated: 2025-12-07
    10→---
    11→
    12→# Technology Stack Recommendations
    13→
    14→This document provides technology recommendations for building a fleet maintenance scheduling system, with rationale for each choice.
    15→
    16→## Stack Overview
    17→
    18→```typescript
    19→interface RecommendedStack {
    20→  backend: {
    21→    primary: 'TypeScript + Node.js (Bun runtime)';
    22→    solver: 'Python (HiGHS + OR-Tools)';
    23→    api: 'Hono framework';
    24→  };
    25→
    26→  database: {
    27→    primary: 'PostgreSQL 16+';
    28→    timeSeries: 'TimescaleDB extension';
    29→    cache: 'Redis';
    30→  };
    31→
    32→  frontend: {
    33→    framework: 'React 18+ with TypeScript';
    34→    meta: 'Next.js or Astro';
    35→    visualization: 'ECharts';
    36→  };
    37→
    38→  infrastructure: {
    39→    containers: 'Docker';
    40→    orchestration: 'Docker Compose or Kubernetes';
    41→    cloud: 'AWS/GCP/Azure agnostic';
    42→  };
    43→}
    44→```
    45→
    46→## Backend: TypeScript + Bun
    47→
    48→### Why TypeScript
    49→
    50→```typescript
    51→interface TypeScriptBenefits {
    52→  typeSafety: 'Catch errors at compile time';
    53→  developer: 'Excellent IDE support, refactoring';
    54→  ecosystem: 'Largest package ecosystem (npm)';
    55→  hiring: 'Large developer pool';
    56→  fullStack: 'Same language frontend and backend';
    57→}
    58→```
    59→
    60→### Why Bun Runtime
    61→
    62→```typescript
    63→interface BunBenefits {
    64→  performance: '3-4x faster than Node.js';
    65→  native: 'TypeScript without transpilation';
    66→  bundler: 'Built-in bundler and test runner';
    67→  compatibility: 'Node.js API compatible';
    68→  modern: 'ESM-first, Web APIs built-in';
    69→}
    70→```
    71→
    72→### Setup Example
    73→
    74→```bash
    75→# Initialize Bun project
    76→bun init
    77→
    78→# Install dependencies
    79→bun add hono drizzle-orm postgres zod
    80→bun add -d typescript @types/node
    81→```
    82→
    83→```typescript
    84→// src/index.ts
    85→import { Hono } from 'hono';
    86→import { cors } from 'hono/cors';
    87→import { logger } from 'hono/logger';
    88→
    89→const app = new Hono();
    90→
    91→app.use('*', logger());
    92→app.use('*', cors());
    93→
    94→app.get('/health', (c) => c.json({ status: 'ok' }));
    95→
    96→export default {
    97→  port: process.env.PORT ?? 3000,
    98→  fetch: app.fetch
    99→};
   100→```
   101→
   102→## Solver: Python
   103→
   104→### Why Python for Solver
   105→
   106→```typescript
   107→interface PythonSolverBenefits {
   108→  libraries: 'Best optimization library support';
   109→  highs: 'Native highspy bindings';
   110→  ortools: 'Official Google OR-Tools support';
   111→  scientific: 'NumPy, Pandas for data processing';
   112→  academic: 'Optimization research uses Python';
   113→}
   114→```
   115→
   116→### Integration Pattern
   117→
   118→```typescript
   119→// TypeScript calls Python solver via subprocess
   120→import { spawn } from 'bun';
   121→
   122→interface SolverInput {
   123→  events: MaintenanceEvent[];
   124→  periods: number[];
   125→  capacity: number;
   126→  timeLimit: number;
   127→}
   128→
   129→interface SolverOutput {
   130→  status: string;
   131→  schedule: Record<string, number>;
   132→  objective: number;
   133→  solveTime: number;
   134→}
   135→
   136→async function callSolver(input: SolverInput): Promise<SolverOutput> {
   137→  const proc = spawn(['python3', 'solver/main.py'], {
   138→    stdin: 'pipe',
   139→    stdout: 'pipe',
   140→    stderr: 'pipe'
   141→  });
   142→
   143→  // Send input
   144→  proc.stdin.write(JSON.stringify(input));
   145→  proc.stdin.end();
   146→
   147→  // Read output
   148→  const stdout = await new Response(proc.stdout).text();
   149→  const stderr = await new Response(proc.stderr).text();
   150→
   151→  const exitCode = await proc.exited;
   152→  if (exitCode !== 0) {
   153→    throw new Error(`Solver failed: ${stderr}`);
   154→  }
   155→
   156→  return JSON.parse(stdout);
   157→}
   158→```
   159→
   160→```python
   161→# solver/main.py
   162→import sys
   163→import json
   164→import highspy
   165→
   166→def solve(input_data: dict) -> dict:
   167→    # Build and solve model
   168→    h = highspy.Highs()
   169→    h.silent()
   170→
   171→    # ... model building ...
   172→
   173→    h.run()
   174→
   175→    return {
   176→        'status': str(h.getModelStatus()),
   177→        'schedule': extract_schedule(h),
   178→        'objective': h.getInfo().objective_function_value,
   179→        'solveTime': h.getInfo().run_time
   180→    }
   181→
   182→if __name__ == '__main__':
   183→    input_data = json.load(sys.stdin)
   184→    result = solve(input_data)
   185→    print(json.dumps(result))
   186→```
   187→
   188→## Database: PostgreSQL
   189→
   190→### Why PostgreSQL
   191→
   192→```typescript
   193→interface PostgreSQLBenefits {
   194→  reliability: 'Battle-tested, ACID compliant';
   195→  features: 'JSON, arrays, CTEs, window functions';
   196→  extensions: 'TimescaleDB, PostGIS, pg_cron';
   197→  performance: 'Excellent query optimizer';
   198→  operational: 'Well-understood operations';
   199→}
   200→```
   201→
   202→### Key Extensions
   203→
   204→```sql
   205→-- TimescaleDB for time-series metrics
   206→CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS timescaledb;
   207→
   208→-- Enable for mileage history table
   209→SELECT create_hypertable('mileage_readings', 'recorded_at');
   210→
   211→-- pg_cron for scheduled jobs
   212→CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_cron;
   213→
   214→-- Schedule daily RUC calculation
   215→SELECT cron.schedule('daily-ruc', '0 5 * * *',
   216→  $$SELECT calculate_ruc()$$
   217→);
   218→```
   219→
   220→### Schema Example
   221→
   222→```typescript
   223→// drizzle/schema.ts
   224→import { pgTable, uuid, text, integer, timestamp, real } from 'drizzle-orm/pg-core';
   225→
   226→export const assets = pgTable('assets', {
   227→  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
   228→  externalId: text('external_id').unique(),
   229→  name: text('name').notNull(),
   230→  assetType: text('asset_type').notNull(),
   231→  currentMileage: integer('current_mileage').default(0),
   232→  status: text('status').default('active'),
   233→  createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
   234→  updatedAt: timestamp('updated_at').defaultNow()
   235→});
   236→
   237→export const maintenanceTypes = pgTable('maintenance_types', {
   238→  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
   239→  name: text('name').notNull(),
   240→  intervalMileage: integer('interval_mileage'),
   241→  intervalDays: integer('interval_days'),
   242→  durationHours: real('duration_hours').notNull(),
   243→  manHours: real('man_hours').notNull()
   244→});
   245→
   246→export const scheduledEvents = pgTable('scheduled_events', {
   247→  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
   248→  assetId: uuid('asset_id').references(() => assets.id),
   249→  maintenanceTypeId: uuid('maintenance_type_id').references(() => maintenanceTypes.id),
   250→  scheduledWeek: integer('scheduled_week').notNull(),
   251→  targetMileage: integer('target_mileage'),
   252→  status: text('status').default('scheduled'),
   253→  scheduleVersion: integer('schedule_version').notNull()
   254→});
   255→```
   256→
   257→## Cache: Redis
   258→
   259→### Why Redis
   260→
   261→```typescript
   262→interface RedisBenefits {
   263→  speed: 'Microsecond latency';
   264→  versatile: 'Cache, queue, pub/sub, sessions';
   265→  simple: 'Easy to operate';
   266→  scalable: 'Cluster mode for HA';
   267→}
   268→```
   269→
   270→### Use Cases
   271→
   272→```typescript
   273→// Session caching
   274→await redis.set(`session:${userId}`, JSON.stringify(session), 'EX', 3600);
   275→
   276→// API response caching
   277→const cached = await redis.get(`schedule:${scheduleId}`);
   278→if (cached) return JSON.parse(cached);
   279→
   280→// Job queue
   281→await redis.lpush('solver:jobs', JSON.stringify(job));
   282→
   283→// Pub/sub for real-time updates
   284→redis.publish('schedule:updated', JSON.stringify({ scheduleId }));
   285→```
   286→
   287→## Frontend: React + Next.js
   288→
   289→### Why React
   290→
   291→```typescript
   292→interface ReactBenefits {
   293→  ecosystem: 'Largest component ecosystem';
   294→  hiring: 'Most developers know React';
   295→  tooling: 'Excellent development tools';
   296→  flexibility: 'Works with any backend';
   297→}
   298→```
   299→
   300→### Why Next.js
   301→
   302→```typescript
   303→interface NextJSBenefits {
   304→  ssr: 'Server-side rendering for SEO and performance';
   305→  routing: 'File-based routing';
   306→  api: 'API routes for BFF pattern';
   307→  optimization: 'Image, font, script optimization';
   308→  deployment: 'Easy deployment to Vercel or self-host';
   309→}
   310→```
   311→
   312→### Project Structure
   313→
   314→```
   315→apps/web/
   316→├── src/
   317→│   ├── app/                    # Next.js app router
   318→│   │   ├── layout.tsx
   319→│   │   ├── page.tsx            # Dashboard
   320→│   │   ├── schedule/
   321→│   │   │   └── page.tsx        # Schedule view
   322→│   │   └── assets/
   323→│   │       └── page.tsx        # Asset list
   324→│   ├── components/
   325→│   │   ├── ui/                 # Shadcn/ui components
   326→│   │   ├── schedule/           # Schedule-specific
   327→│   │   └── charts/             # Visualization
   328→│   └── lib/
   329→│       ├── api.ts              # API client
   330→│       └── hooks/              # Custom hooks
   331→├── next.config.js
   332→└── package.json
   333→```
   334→
   335→## Visualization: ECharts
   336→
   337→### Why ECharts
   338→
   339→```typescript
   340→interface EChartsBenefits {
   341→  performance: 'Handles 100k+ data points';
   342→  features: 'Gantt, heatmaps, calendars';
   343→  customization: 'Highly configurable';
   344→  opensource: 'Apache 2.0 license';
   345→}
   346→```
   347→
   348→### Schedule Visualization Example
   349→
   350→```typescript
   351→import ReactECharts from 'echarts-for-react';
   352→
   353→function ScheduleGantt({ events }: { events: ScheduledEvent[] }) {
   354→  const option = {
   355→    tooltip: { trigger: 'item' },
   356→    xAxis: {
   357→      type: 'time',
   358→      min: startDate,
   359→      max: endDate
   360→    },
   361→    yAxis: {
   362→      type: 'category',
   363→      data: assets.map(a => a.name)
   364→    },
   365→    series: [{
   366→      type: 'custom',
   367→      renderItem: renderGanttBar,
   368→      encode: { x: [0, 1], y: 2 },
   369→      data: events.map(e => [
   370→        e.startDate,
   371→        e.endDate,
   372→        e.assetIndex,
   373→        e.maintenanceType
   374→      ])
   375→    }]
   376→  };
   377→
   378→  return <ReactECharts option={option} style={{ height: 600 }} />;
   379→}
   380→```
   381→
   382→## Infrastructure
   383→
   384→### Docker Setup
   385→
   386→```dockerfile
   387→# Dockerfile for API
   388→FROM oven/bun:1 AS base
   389→WORKDIR /app
   390→
   391→FROM base AS deps
   392→COPY package.json bun.lockb ./
   393→RUN bun install --frozen-lockfile
   394→
   395→FROM base AS runner
   396→COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
   397→COPY . .
   398→EXPOSE 3000
   399→CMD ["bun", "run", "src/index.ts"]
   400→```
   401→
   402→```yaml
   403→# docker-compose.yml
   404→services:
   405→  api:
   406→    build: ./apps/api
   407→    ports:
   408→      - "3000:3000"
   409→    environment:
   410→      - DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:postgres@db:5432/mms
   411→      - REDIS_URL=redis://redis:6379
   412→    depends_on:
   413→      - db
   414→      - redis
   415→
   416→  solver:
   417→    build: ./packages/solver
   418→    environment:
   419→      - REDIS_URL=redis://redis:6379
   420→
   421→  db:
   422→    image: timescale/timescaledb:latest-pg16
   423→    volumes:
   424→      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data
   425→    environment:
   426→      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=postgres
   427→      - POSTGRES_DB=mms
   428→
   429→  redis:
   430→    image: redis:7-alpine
   431→    volumes:
   432→      - redisdata:/data
   433→
   434→volumes:
   435→  pgdata:
   436→  redisdata:
   437→```
   438→
   439→## Package Structure
   440→
   441→### Monorepo Layout
   442→
   443→```
   444→/
   445→├── apps/
   446→│   ├── api/                    # TypeScript API server
   447→│   └── web/                    # Next.js frontend
   448→├── packages/
   449→│   ├── solver/                 # Python solver
   450→│   ├── shared/                 # Shared TypeScript types
   451→│   └── db/                     # Database schema & migrations
   452→├── docker-compose.yml
   453→├── package.json                # Workspace root
   454→└── turbo.json                  # Turborepo config
   455→```
   456→
   457→## Next Steps
   458→
   459→With tech stack selected:
   460→
   461→- Study `02-data-model.md` for database design
   462→- Explore `03-api-design.md` for API patterns
   463→- Review `06-FEATURES/` for feature implementation
   464→
